context_note: Belgium vs Egypt - Belgium a clear but not overwhelming favorite (FTR 61.19% / Draw 23.47% / Egypt 15.34%, vig-adj). Draw price (23.5%) is below the RULE8 'high draw price' threshold (~25%) but not negligible - moderate competitiveness regime, NOT a GER-CUR-style blowout (FTR <90%) and NOT a JPN-NED-style even-terms match either. For Q1 (fouls comparison), the historical ESPN averages for Belgium (12.2) and Egypt (12.0) are essentially IDENTICAL - raw Skellam already sits near 50% (~0.44) with NO extreme to shrink from, so RULE8/10 shrinkage is largely moot here; applied only a small additional nudge toward 0.50 reflecting RULE10's 'winning team fouls >= losing team' pattern (Belgium favored to win -> if true, would push toward Belgium fouling more / Egypt less, i.e. slightly below 0.50). The corners-handicap market (Belgium -0.5/Egypt +0.5 corners, market 119429083) exists but is COMPLETELY ILLIQUID (zero bids/offers both sides) - cannot be used as a dominance proxy for Q1, unlike SWE-TUN's Q1 corner-handicap fallback.

mahmoud_hassan_trezeguet_score_or_assist.note: offer-only, illiquid, x0.945 -> 0.245, resolved via contract name 'Mahmoud Hassan' = Trezeguet's real name

no_market_exists_for: total match cards (only team-specific 1.5-card O/U lines)
illiquid_unusable: belgium_minus0.5_corners_egypt_plus0.5_corners (119429083) - zero bids/offers both sides, cannot serve as Q1 dominance proxy

q1_egypt_more_fouls_than_belgium.method: Skellam on historical foul rates: Belgium avg=12.2 (n=5, range 9-20), Egypt avg=12.0 (n=5, range 9-15) - essentially IDENTICAL averages, unlike SWE-TUN/CIV-ECU where one team's average was a clear outlier. mean_diff=-0.2, var=24.2, continuity-corrected P(Egypt>Belgium)=0.443 - raw signal is already near 50% with no extreme to shrink FROM. No related dominance market exists (corners handicap illiquid). Per RULE10's 'winning team fouls >= losing team' pattern (n=2: GER-CUR, SWE-TUN), Belgium being the clear favorite (61.2%) provides a weak additional nudge toward Belgium fouling more / Egypt fouling less (i.e. slightly below the raw 0.443). Landed at 0.46 - within the 0.40-0.60 'genuinely uncertain' band the task flagged, reflecting low confidence either direction.

q2_2plus_total_cards_2h.method: No direct 2H cards market exists. Smarkets team-specific full-match O/U 1.5 cards markets give market-implied Poisson lambdas: Belgium ~1.42 (from Over1.5=41.94%), Egypt ~2.08 (from Over1.5=61.52%), combined full-match lambda~3.50. ESPN full-match historical cards (yellow+red): Belgium avg=1.4, Egypt avg=1.2, combined avg~2.6 - market is somewhat higher than ESPN history, used market as primary (sharper, current-team signal) with mild downweight toward ESPN. Blended full-match lambda~3.2, scaled by 0.6 for 2H (most cards occur in 2H) -> 2H lambda~1.92. P(X>=2) for Poisson(1.92) = 1-e^-1.92*(1+1.92) = 1-0.1466*2.92 = 0.572. Rounded to 0.60 reflecting the market's slightly higher implied rate (pure market-only calc gave 0.62).

q3_btts_and_3plus_goals.method: Direct from Smarkets 'Both teams to score and over/under 2.5' market, 'Yes/Over' contract (BTTS=Yes AND total goals Over 2.5 = 3+), vig-adjusted 37.61%. Undeviated.

q4_egypt_more_2h_sot_than_belgium.method: ESPN full-match SOT: Belgium avg=8.2 (n=5, inflated by blowouts vs Liechtenstein/Tunisia/USA), Egypt avg=3.2 (n=5). 2H-scaled Skellam (lambda_Belgium=4.1, lambda_Egypt=1.6): mean_diff=-2.5, var=5.7, continuity-corrected P(Egypt>Belgium)=0.104. Submitted near-raw per RULE7 (unshaded first-pass models win) - Belgium's attacking output has been historically dominant, Egypt's SOT props consistently low.

q5_4plus_total_2h_sot.method: Sum of 2H-scaled SOT lambdas: Belgium=4.1 + Egypt=1.6 = 5.7. Poisson P(X>=4) = 1 - [P(0)+P(1)+P(2)+P(3)] = 1 - (0.00334+0.01904+0.05426+0.1031) = 1-0.1797 = 0.820. High estimate driven by Belgium's very high recent SOT volume (avg 8.2/match, several blowout wins). Submitted near-raw.

q6_belgium_2plus_offsides.method: First-pass Poisson, Belgium offsides n=5 (1,3,1,2,2), lambda=1.8, P(X>=2)=1-e^-1.8*(1+1.8)=1-0.1653*2.8=0.537. Submitted unshaded per RULE7 (this category's best-performing pattern: BRA-MAR +20.36, GER-CUR +13.79, JPN-NED +12.02).

q7_belgium_win.method: Direct from Smarkets full-time-result market, vig-adjusted (Belgium 61.19%). Treated as tier1_market undeviated per established convention (RULE1/2).

q8_ht_tied.method: Direct from Smarkets 'Half-time result' market, Draw contract, vig-adjusted 40.80%. Undeviated.

q9_tielemans_1plus_sot.method: Direct from Smarkets 'Player to have over 0.5 shots on target' market, Youri Tielemans contract resolved cleanly via name field. Offer-only 40.00% (illiquid, no bid), x0.945 ratio -> 0.378. Tielemans is a deep-lying Belgium midfielder but in a match Belgium is heavily favored to dominate possession/territory.

q10_trezeguet_score_or_assist.method: Direct from Smarkets 'Player to score or assist' market. Trezeguet's contract is listed under his real name 'Mahmoud Hassan' (full name Mahmoud Abdelmonem Hassan 'Trezeguet') - resolved via name field. Offer-only 25.97% (illiquid, no bid), x0.945 ratio -> 0.245. Trezeguet is Egypt's veteran wide attacker/captain, a reasonable focal-attacker prior for the underdog's primary outlet.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will Egypt commit more fouls than Belgium?
questions.note: EXACT TIE: Belgium 15 - Egypt 15. Our near-50/50 estimate (driven by near-identical ESPN averages, 12.2 vs 12.0) landed almost exactly on the actual outcome's underlying mechanism - this is the SECOND time 'near-identical historical averages' has produced a literal tie (after JPN-NED's 7-7). Validates: when both teams' recent foul averages are close with no outlier, stay near 50% - it's not a hedge, it's often the right call.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will there be 2 or more total cards shown in the second half?
questions.note: 4 total cards (2-2), but 3 of 4 were in 1H (13',14',34') and only 1 in 2H (75'). Both us and crowd leaned YES and were both wrong, but us was marginally closer.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will both teams score AND the match have 3 or more total goals?
questions.note: BTTS=YES (1-1, Belgium's goal via OG) but total goals=2 < 3. tier1_market undeviated won.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will Egypt have more shots on target than Belgium in the second half?
questions.note: Final SOT: Belgium 3 - Egypt 3 (EXACT TIE, not the blowout our model implied from Belgium's 8.2 avg). 'More than' on a tie = NO either way, so our extreme 0.11 (predicting Belgium dominance) still landed on the correct side even though the underlying mechanism (tie, not dominance) differed from the model's assumption. Egypt's defense suppressed BELGIUM's output down to Egypt's own level across SOT, offsides, etc - see summary.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will there be 4 or more total shots on target in the second half?
questions.note: Total match SOT=6 (3+3); enough landed in 2H to clear the 4+ bar. High unshaded estimate (RULE7) won.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will Belgium be caught offside 2 or more times?
questions.note: Belgium had ZERO offsides (vs recent avg 1.8) - outcome=NO again, now 5/5 for this prop family (BRA-MAR/GER-CUR/JPN-NED/CIV-ECU/BEL-EGY all NO). This is the FIRST time the unshaded Poisson came out >0.50 (0.54, lambda=1.8) and it's the FIRST (small) loss in this family. Watch item: 'X 2+ offsides' may skew lower than Poisson(recent avg) suggests - consider a small downward nudge when P>0.50, though the family's net record (+54.57/5=+10.91 avg) still strongly favors RULE7's unshaded approach.
Belgium drew 1-1 (did not win) - a moderate upset vs the 61.2% market price. Crowd over-credited Belgium (0.66) even more than the market did; tier1_market-deferred (0.61) beat crowd again (RULE1).

questions.leaderboard_question: At halftime, will the match be tied?
questions.note: Egypt led 1-0 at HT (Ashour, 19'). tier1_market undeviated won by a hair.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will Youri Tielemans have at least 1 shot on target?
questions.note: Tielemans 0 SOT (consistent with Belgium's overall SOT collapse to 3). Liquid-ish market price beat crowd.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will Mahmoud Trezeguet score or assist a goal (excluding own goals)?
questions.note: No score/assist for Trezeguet (Egypt's goal was Ashour's, the equalizer was an OG). Both us and crowd close to actual; us marginally better.

post_match_results.summary: Belgium 1-1 Egypt - our 2nd-best match ever (+63.72, 9/10 beat crowd, only Q6 below crowd by a tiny -1.57). Egypt (15.3% underdog) scored early (19', Ashour) and led at HT; Belgium's equalizer was an EGYPTIAN OWN GOAL (66'). The headline pattern: EGYPT'S DEFENSE SUPPRESSED BELGIUM'S OUTPUT DOWN TO EGYPT'S OWN TYPICAL LEVEL across nearly every stat - fouls tied exactly 15-15 (vs recent averages 12.2/12.0, nearly identical to begin with), SOT tied exactly 3-3 (Belgium's avg is 8.2 - their output collapsed by more than half), offsides Belgium=0 (vs avg 1.8). This is arguably the MIRROR of RULE10 (blowout: loser's stats collapse toward winner's level) - here, a RESILIENT UNDERDOG caused the FAVORITE's stats to collapse toward the UNDERDOG's level, even though the match was only a draw (not a blowout for the underdog). Two 'near-identical historical averages -> stay near 50%' calls (Q1) produced an EXACT TIE for the second time (after JPN-NED). Q6 (Belgium 2+ offsides) is the 5th data point for that prop family and the first loss - all 5 have resolved NO, but this is the first time our unshaded Poisson exceeded 0.50.
